data.body:
TRANSITION REMEDIATION COMPLETE (TS1-4 done, closed, all 8 Gerrit changes LLM-Review+1 AND Verified+1), but EPIC completion-verifier FAILS on 3 ACs BEYOND the 4-delta remediation scope — NOT covered by any transition story:
- AC1: no write-time guard rejecting PRIVATE-KEY material in an identity event (missing since original ddbe/change 558). Small security hardening.
- AC7: enforcement CLI diverges from spec — delivered 'rebar verify-authorship' (config-driven, TEXT output) vs AC7's 'rebar verify-identity --require-authenticated --since <identity.enforce_since>' with JSON report entries {event_uuid,ticket_id,commit,author,verdict,display}, cutover grandfathering, and a CI workflow file. Spirit met (opt-in gate, exits non-zero, replay-never-rejects, fsck/show grouping) but letter unbuilt. Substantial.
- AC10: key_not_valid_at_era must be added to verify_signature_result.schema.json per the AC; TS3 instead kept it as a separate verify-authorship verdict vocabulary (the TS3 code-review flagged the shared schema as the 'wrong command'). Design tension.
These 3 were pre-existing gaps in the DELIVERED stack, outside the 4 flagged deltas (authorship-payload/era-model/ledger/verdict). NOT submitting the stack pending a scope decision: (A) land the 4 green fixes now + file AC1/AC7/AC10 as follow-on stories; (B) build AC1/AC7/AC10 before landing; (C) reconcile/waive over-specified AC7 sub-parts + do the small AC1+AC10. Awaiting direction.
